transcript.clean_text: foreign the biggest weekend of the Tour de France is the race hits the Alps it's live and On Demand on gcn Plus the day after in your screen it is it won a big stage to con Colombia it was surely going to be a general classification fight with the Riders turning up to face off against each other and mass in the Alps to mozin a 20th stage finish down the years a Tour de France classic with some big mountains in the way the biggest of which coming as the final climb of the day the colder plan where bonus seconds eight five and two for first second and third were available in the general classification a dive down to mozin for a stage finish that was last used in 2016 with Spanish Runner Yoni seguire winning in a thunderstorm back then the slope's horrible The Descent difficult it was going to be a big day just under 160 kilometers then but it started with a bad bad moment a mass crash forced a neutralization of the race lots of riders down some eventually having to pull out after that crash the race continued Julio Ciccone going on to make the early Breakaway never really got much time he take points and he'd move up into second place in the king of the mountains competition he however would be caught by yombo visma who were convinced that they had to make it difficult for everybody and that they were doing going up the penultimate climb of the day one by one their Riders caused pain and damage Bookman losing contact and in the top 10 as well Tom pidcock was struggling to hold on Watford was putting pain into the legs of those behind and even causing problems briefly for Simon Yates Yates would have to work hard to get back on with Felix gal and guilla Mata his teammate Chris Harper doing a great job for him and bringing him back with 30 kilometers to go 30 kilometers in the final climb 16 Riders to take on the first few slopes and it wouldn't take long before they were the first casualties fanart as well as Raphael Micah and then Bilbao and Simon Yates lost contact as there were less than half of the 16 at the front now I think you're always there Bugatti with him we were down to six Henley was the next to be distanced the third place in play with Carlos Rodriguez hanging on and then sekus budged out of the way by Adam Yates then there were three Yates setting up today pogacha and the attack would come with 3.7 kilometers still to the top acha opened up a gap linga Gore was gonna have to work for it but work he did to his own power to his own Rhythm and he'd come back inside the last kilometer and a half ready to sit on the Wheel and was ready to go with the bonus seconds available a motorbike got in the way however and then viago was able to go and take the bonuses eight seconds to him five going to bulgacha so a three second swing starting the day nine seconds of a difference it now became 12 in favor of the defending Champion Carlos Rodriguez would work his way on and then go straight over the top that whilst the favorites were looking at each other a five second descent difference and it became enough to look like he'd have a chance to win the stage but Adam Yates was back not enough though to catch Rodriguez a 22 year old from southern Spain he'd never won abroad this though was to be the biggest moment of his career Rodriguez making it a third Spanish stage win at this particular tour second place to pugacha taking two bonus seconds back on mingogor the sums at the end of the day were one extra second to the Dane who now led by 10. a stage win and now a Podium place in the GC for Carlos Rodriguez all the way from Andalusia to the Alps and beating pogacha and vinegar I look on and they'll fight another day slight change in the GC but no change in the leader a 20th yellow Jersey for Jonas Vigor who now leads by 10 seconds from tare pogacha Rodriguez up onto the podium by one single second ahead of jai hindley who will hope to fight to bring it back a change in the wear of the polka dot Jersey as well Vega is level on points with powerless but takes over the lead this is what's to come on stage 15. from legit to San Jose first category climbs are plenty including the final climb to San Jose another tough day this time with distance as well just under 180 kilometers Stage 15 will close up the second block of racing at the store could go a long way to deciding the destination of the final yellow Jersey in just over a week's time
